scores.correctness_notes: The agent correctly identifies IngressPerAppRequirer and uses v2, which aligns with the gold standard. However, the gold standard focuses on the data schema (application-data: name, model, port; unit-data: host) which the agent omits entirely. The gold standard also notes that charmlibs docs cover interface specs/data schemas rather than Python API — the agent answers a different dimension than the gold standard.
scores.hallucination_notes: The agent provides many specific API details not confirmed by the gold standard: constructor parameters strip_prefix, redirect_https, scheme; methods is_ready() and provide_ingress_requirements(); event attribute event.url on IngressPerAppReadyEvent. Only IngressPerAppRequirer itself is mentioned in the gold standard. Some of these details (e.g., is_ready()) may not exist in the actual library.
